steps.title: Identify when the equation fails to have exactly one solution
steps.content:
For $x(r - 29) = 24$ to have exactly one solution, the coefficient of $x$ must be nonzero.

When $r - 29 = 0$ (i.e., $r = 29$):
$$x(0) = 24$$
This becomes $0 = 24$, which is impossible.

All other values of $r$ keep the coefficient nonzero, giving exactly one solution.

Therefore, $r = 29$ cannot be the value. That is the correct answer.
